{"query":"Tesla Corpus Christi lithium refinery annual production capacity","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1639,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://electrek.co/2026/01/15/tesla-rare-look-lithium-refinery-confirms-production-start/","title":"Tesla releases rare look at its lithium refinery, confirms production start | Electrek","content":"Tesla has released a new video giving us a closer look at its massive lithium refinery in Corpus Christi, Texas, and confirming that the facility has officially started production.
The automaker broke ground on its own refinery in Robstown, Texas, near Corpus Christi, in 2023 with the goal of deploying a new, more efficient refining process.
Now, just two years later, Tesla is showing off the operational plant.
“From breaking ground in 2023 to running rock through the kiln in 2024 to start a full integrated plant startup now in 2025.”
(Note: While the video was released this week, the “now in 2025” comment implies the facility likely reached full integrated startup late last year).
Bevan highlights that this is the “first spodumene to lithium hydroxide refinery in North America,” marking a significant shift in the region’s battery supply chain.
Output: It moves to crystallization to produce battery-grade lithium hydroxide.
“Our process is more sustainable than traditional methods and eliminates hazardous byproducts,” Bevan said.
We have been tracking this project closely since the groundbreaking, and seeing it go from a dirt lot to a fully operational refinery in roughly two years is impressive.
Check out our YouTube channel for the latest reviews."},{"url":"https://www.greendrive-accessories.com/blog/language/en/tesla-achieves-major-milestone-with-texas-lithium-refinery/","title":"Tesla Achieves Major Milestone with Texas Lithium Refinery","content":"Tesla broke ground on the refinery in May 2023, with construction valued at approximately $375 million.
The crew fed raw materials through the refinery’s kiln for the first time.
Increasing lithium refining capacity is critical to a sustainable energy economy.”
It is the first large-scale U.S. facility for battery-grade lithium refining.
The project underscores Tesla’s efforts to increase the supply of battery-grade lithium in North America.
Tesla’s Texas refinery will convert spodumene concentrate, a lithium-rich material, into battery-grade lithium hydroxide.
The start of operations is a significant accomplishment, but it is just the beginning.
Tesla plans to increase output at the site in the coming months.
Once fully operational, the refinery is estimated to produce around 50 GWh of battery-grade lithium annually.
This output will directly assist Tesla’s expanding vehicle delivery and battery production goals.
The inauguration of Tesla’s Texas refinery aligns with larger initiatives in the United States and Europe to lessen reliance on China for EV battery supplies.
As production increases, the facility will play an important part in meeting Tesla’s ambitious EV and energy targets."},{"url":"https://evcube.net/tesla-corpus-christi-lithium-plant-2026/","title":"Tesla's Corpus Christi Lithium Plant: Inside the 2026 Buildout","date":"14 hours ago","content":"14 hours ago — At full capacity, about 50,000 tonnes of lithium carbonate equivalent per year — roughly 50 GWh, enough for around one million electric ..."}]}
